name: Vigo
description_short: Western Union agent network providing money transfer, cash pickup, and bill payment services through 46+ Colorado locations.
description_long:
Western Union is a global money transfer and financial services company that operates through a network of agents worldwide, including 46+ locations across Colorado. The company has been facilitating cross-border and domestic money transfers for decades, establishing itself as a primary alternative financial services provider for unbanked and underbanked consumers.

The Colorado agent network offers several core services accessible through their website locator tool at locations.vigoglobal.com. Consumers can send money domestically and internationally, pick up cash transfers at agent locations, track transfers in real-time, receive money directly to cards, pay bills, manage mobile top-ups, and access Western Union Rewards programs. The service operates through partnerships with retail agents including grocery stores, check-cashing shops, and specialty retailers across the state.

Western Union's Colorado agent locations distinguish themselves through extended accessibility—many agents are embedded in immigrant-serving retailers and community markets (evident from location names like Castaneda's Mexican Market, Centro Hispano, and Envios El Punto), providing culturally relevant access points. The company offers multilingual support (English, Spanish, Haitian Creole, Chinese, French, Vietnamese) and operates through a digital-first interface with mobile functionality for tracking and estimation.

However, Western Union's service model relies entirely on third-party agent locations with variable hours (opening times range 8 AM–11 AM), and consumers have no direct bank relationship with the company. Fees for money transfers are not transparent on the locator website itself, requiring users to estimate pricing separately. The service is transactional rather than relationship-based, offering no account management, credit-building features, or financial products beyond transfer and bill payment.
address: Dong Dong Inc, 88 E Broadway, New York, NY 10002

company_info.headquarters: Dong Dong Inc, 88 E Broadway, New York, NY 10002

pros: 46+ agent locations across Colorado enabling widespread cash pickup access
Multilingual platform support in 6 languages (English, Spanish, Haitian Creole, Chinese, French, Vietnamese)
Real-time transfer tracking and price estimation tools available online
Extended service menu including bill payment, mobile top-ups, and inmate deposits
Integration with retail agents in underserved communities and immigrant-serving markets
Western Union Rewards loyalty program for frequent senders
Ability to receive money directly to prepaid cards without traditional bank account
cons: Variable agent hours (earliest opening at 8 AM, latest at 11 AM) create access friction
No transparent fee pricing displayed on locator website—requires separate price estimation step
Reliance on third-party agent locations means inconsistent service quality across network
No account-based relationship, credit reporting, or credit-building features
Service limited to transactional transfers rather than ongoing financial management
best_for: Immigrants and families needing frequent international remittance services
Unbanked and underbanked consumers without access to traditional bank accounts
Consumers in rural or underserved Colorado communities near agent locations
Individuals requiring bill payment and money transfer in a single location
diagnosis: Western Union is best for immigrants, unbanked consumers, and families needing remittance or money transfer services across Colorado's agent network. The primary caveat is complete reliance on third-party agents with variable hours and no direct banking relationship—this is a transaction service, not a financial account, with fees that require separate estimation.
services: Money receipt directly to prepaid cards
